title: Improve merge reliability: resolve strategy and configurable conflict cap
description:
Two related issues make sequential merges of parallel feature branches painful:

## Problem 1: Criss-cross merge phantom conflicts

When a conflict-resolution bead merges main into a feature branch and then takt merge tries to merge the feature branch back into main, git's default 'ort' strategy sees a criss-cross topology and generates phantom conflicts on lines that don't actually differ. The workaround ('git merge -s resolve') works but is not available through takt merge.

Fix: in src/agent_takt/gitutils.py, WorktreeManager.merge_branch(), pass '-s' 'resolve' as additional arguments to the git merge command. This uses the simpler 'resolve' strategy which picks a single merge base and avoids criss-cross phantom conflicts.

Current code (gitutils.py ~line 94):
  self._run_git('merge', '--no-ff', branch_name, '-m', f'Merge {branch_name}')

Fix:
  self._run_git('merge', '--no-ff', '-s', 'resolve', branch_name, '-m', f'Merge {branch_name}')

## Problem 2: Corrective attempt cap too low

config.scheduler.max_corrective_attempts defaults to 2. When main advances quickly (CI commits, multiple sequential feature merges), 2 conflict resolution attempts are not enough and the system bails out with 'Manual operator intervention required' rather than retrying.

Fix: increase the default value of max_corrective_attempts from 2 to 5 in src/agent_takt/config.py. Also verify it is documented in .takt/config.yaml comments or the config dataclass docstring.

## Files to modify
- src/agent_takt/gitutils.py: add '-s', 'resolve' to merge_branch()
- src/agent_takt/config.py: change default max_corrective_attempts from 2 to 5

## Acceptance criteria
- WorktreeManager.merge_branch() uses '-s resolve' in the git merge invocation
- Default max_corrective_attempts is 5
- All existing tests pass
- No regressions in merge_branch tests or scheduler corrective-attempt tests
